sections.paragraph:
αὐτομολέω (αὐτός, cp. 2 aor. μολεῖν ‘go’) fut. 3 sg. αὐτομολήσει Pr 6:11a (A); 1 aor. ηὐτομόλησα LXX (Hdt., Aristoph. et al.; PGissUniv 46 IV 30; Philo, Aet. M. 76; Jos., Bell. 4, 380, Vi. 107; 239) desert ἀπό τινος ( fr.) someone 1 Cl 28:2.—DELG s.v. αὐτός 1 and βλώσκω (aor. ἔμολον).

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

aor. aor. = aorist

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PGissUniv PGissUniv = Mitteilungen aus der Papyrussammlung der Giessener Universitätsbibliothek—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. fr. = from

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 152
